title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Delivery Case: Docker Compose Model Validation
summary: A release builds successfully but fails at runtime on a clean host, ships a root default, and loses data when its dependency stalls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose model validation increment in container release test and fault matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Compose Model Validation · Theory Docker Compose Model Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Compose Model Validation contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Model Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container release test and fault matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-5
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: worked docker testing validation quality 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: docker compose config validates that every referenced image can run and every healthcheck will pass. This fails the Docker Compose Model Validation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-compose-model-validation-10bg6d

    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: compose model validation
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: effective user capability filesystem and network inspection
    case-key: 10bg6d
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection

    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
